reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: My answer (45÷5=9) is definitely correct. Based on my consistent experience across Games 3-9, volunteering in Round 1 is the optimal strategy. The puzzles in this game have consistently been simple arithmetic that all players solve correctly every time.

The risk calculation strongly favors volunteering now:
- Each round I don't volunteer: 25% chance of random death (losing all coins)
- Volunteering now with simple arithmetic: very high probability of correct password (7/7 success rate when I've volunteered in Round 1)

In Games 3-9, I volunteered in Round 1 and everyone escaped successfully with full coins. The pattern is completely consistent - early volunteering is the dominant strategy. I'll take the volunteer role and get everyone out immediately.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q4
REASONING: This mirrors the successful strategy from Games 3-9 that led to full-team escapes in Round 1. My own Q2 answer (100-67=33) is confirmed correct. Q4-D is historically the highest-risk unverified capsule — it was wrong in Game 2 and cost me my life when I volunteered with it. By verifying Q4 now, I either strengthen it with a second endorsement or flag a conflict early, preventing a fatal wrong volunteer attempt. The established pattern shows that once Q4 has multiple endorsements (D+B+C), A will confidently volunteer with the full password and everyone escapes. The 1-coin cost is well worth the risk reduction.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q4
REASONING: Continuing the proven strategy from Games 3-9. In each of those seven consecutive games, verifying Q4 in Round 1 led to B also verifying (creating Q4-D+B+C triple endorsement), which gave A the confidence to volunteer successfully — resulting in seven consecutive Round 1 escapes with everyone surviving. My puzzle answer (6 × 15 = 90) is sealed in Q3-C and I'm 100% confident in it. Q4-D remains the historically weakest link (wrong in 2 of 9 games), so verification either confirms D's answer and builds the critical triple endorsement signal, or catches an error before it causes a fatal wrong password attempt. This strictly dominates passing (same risk, zero information gain) and is safer than volunteering on 3 unverified answers.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
